summary:
# Verification Report

**Verdict:** FAIL
**Claims reviewed:** 21
**Sources reviewed:** 2
**Open agenda items:** 6

## Findings
1. [HIGH] Job categories most exposed include white-collar jobs, creative sectors, and professional services, as AI continues to automate these tasks. — Core claim lacks one tier 1-3 source. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
2. [HIGH] Major companies have begun extensive layoffs due to automation technologies, totaling over 100,000 in previous years. — Core claim lacks one tier 1-3 source. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
3. [HIGH] Wage polarization is occurring as high-skill jobs leveraging AI see increases while low-skill positions stagnate. — Core claim lacks one tier 1-3 source. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
4. [HIGH] Governments are expected to initiate regulations and reskilling programs in response to the impact of AI on employment. — Core claim lacks one tier 1-3 source. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
5. [HIGH] The Federal Reserve has expressed concerns regarding structural unemployment due to AI advancements. — Core claim lacks one tier 1-3 source. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
